# Statuses

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

HTTP status utility for Node.js

This module provides a utility to work with HTTP status codes, messages and types. It includes helper methods to determine if a status code represents an error, redirect, empty response, etc.

## Installation

```bash
$ npm install statuses
```

## API

```js
var status = require('statuses')
```

### status(code)

Returns the numeric status code from a status code or message.

```js
status(403) // => 403
status('403') // => 403
status('Forbidden') // => 403
```

Throws if the status cannot be found.

### status.STATUS_CODES

A map of status codes to status messages, like Node.js's [http.STATUS_CODES](https://nodejs.org/api/http.html#http_http_status_codes).

```js
status.STATUS_CODES[404] // => 'Not Found'
```

### status.codes

Array of all status codes.

```js
status.codes // => [100, 101, 102, ...]
```

### status.empty

An object of status codes that represent an empty body.

```js
status.empty[204] // => true
status.empty[304] // => true
```

### status.redirect

An object of status codes that represent a redirect.

```js
status.redirect[301] // => true
status.redirect[302] // => true
```

### status.retry

An object of status codes that represent when a request should be retried.

```js
status.retry[502] // => true
status.retry[503] // => true
```

### status.isClientError(code)

Returns true if the status code is a client error (4xx).

```js
status.isClientError(404) // => true
status.isClientError(500) // => false
```

### status.isInformational(code)

Returns true if the status code is an informational response (1xx).

```js
status.isInformational(100) // => true
status.isInformational(200) // => false
```

### status.isRedirect(code)

Returns true if the status code is a redirect (3xx).

```js
status.isRedirect(301) // => true
status.isRedirect(200) // => false
```

### status.isServerError(code)

Returns true if the status code is a server error (5xx).

```js
status.isServerError(500) // => true
status.isServerError(400) // => false
```

### status.isSuccess(code)

Returns true if the status code is a success (2xx).

```js
status.isSuccess(200) // => true
status.isSuccess(404) // => false
```

### status.isEmpty(code)

Returns true if the status code represents an empty response body.

```js
status.isEmpty(204) // => true
status.isEmpty(200) // => false
```

### status.isRetryable(code)

Returns true if the status code represents a retryable request.

```js
status.isRetryable(503) // => true
status.isRetryable(500) // => false
```

## Examples

### Get the status text for a code

```js
var status = require('statuses')
var text = status.STATUS_CODES[404] // => 'Not Found'
```

### Check if a request should be retried

```js
var status = require('statuses')

if (status.isRetryable(res.statusCode)) {
  // retry the request after a delay
  setTimeout(sendRequest, 500)
}
```

### Check if a response is empty

```js
var status = require('statuses')

if (status.isEmpty(res.statusCode)) {
  // response has no body, end the request
  req.end()
}
```
